title: Stoneford Precision Works
content:
Stoneford Precision Works is a maritime logistics company founded in 1929 and headquartered in Juniper Reach. Cleo Yarrow served as chief executive during its international expansion phase.

History.

Competitors accused the company of undercutting service contracts, prompting a trade review.

Operations.

Cleo Yarrow negotiated distribution agreements that opened markets in two neighboring republics.

Products.

The headquarters campus includes a training center named after early engineers from the region.

Corporate affairs.

Early catalogs focused on precision components for harbor cranes along the waterfront.

Controversy.

Brand spin-offs now cover software diagnostics sold to municipal transport agencies.

History.

Annual reports show steady revenue growth after diversification into overseas service contracts.

Operations.

The firm's research division partners with universities testing next-generation alloys.

Products.

A factory modernization program in 1989 automated assembly lines for turbine control modules.

Corporate affairs.

Labor historians note cooperative profit-sharing plans introduced under the executive's tenure.

Controversy.

Environmental audits in 2008 led to closed-loop cooling systems at the main plant.

Competitors accused the company of undercutting service contracts, prompting a trade review.
